eventual_consistency_model.description: GitHub Pages operates as an eventually-consistent distributed system. After a gh-pages branch push, content propagates through: (1) GitHub Pages builder → (2) GitHub CDN edge cache → (3) Cloudflare reverse proxy → (4) Custom domain DNS. Each layer has independent propagation latency.

convergence_detection_strategy.approach: Multi-phase active probing with consecutive-pass confirmation
false_positive_prevention: Transient vs permanent failure discrimination (HTTP 5xx vs 404)
3 consecutive clean passes required before declaring convergence
Per-URL retry tracking (not global retry reset)
Exponential backoff with jitter prevents thundering herd
Confidence scoring prevents borderline-pass false positives

phases.phase1: Initial stabilization wait (90s) — lets Pages builder complete
phases.phase2: CDN readiness probing — detects when Cloudflare edge is serving fresh content
phases.phase3: Incremental URL retry — per-URL confirmation with backoff
phases.phase4: Convergence confirmation — 3x consecutive clean passes with 30s gaps
phases.phase5: Historical continuity — verifies old reports not overwritten

anti-patterns_eliminated: Single-pass validation after fixed wait (race condition source)
Global retry on all URLs (hides per-URL permanent failures)
Treating CDN timeouts as permanent failures (false P0s)
Using short fixed wait (30s) for 2-5 minute CDN propagation
